title: planner chooses wrong plan due to the overestimation of equal condition on index
description: ## Bug Report Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks! ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) The reproduce steps are internal only. ### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required) this is the plan of v7.5.6, use topn and use the composite index which can cover the 6 filters(and there are 7 filters total) ### 3. What did you see instead (Required) this is the plan of v8.5.3, use limit and use the primary key, which is also the order by key. you can see the result is empty, which means the IndexFullScan will scan the whole index, which is suboptimal ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required) v8.5.3
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, UNIQUE (name, value));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'test', 10.00);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (2, 'example', 20.50);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name = 'test' AND value = 10.00;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE name IS NULL OR value < 15.00;
key_bug_features: overestimation of equal condition on index
